summary: Apply an already-decided EDL keep-list to the source: the frame-quantized trim/setpts/atempo per-segment render + video-only concat + room-tone crossfade audio graph that turns a decided cut into the spliced film. Mechanics only — WHICH spans to keep and the per-segment speed are decided on the brain and cross as data.

parity.note: The per-segment trim=start/end_frame + setpts + atempo filtergraph, the video-only concat and the room-tone acrossfade audio graph are PURE functions of (keep, speed F, fps_grid, xfade) — all of which are DECISIONS made by the cut brain and arriving as params. libx264 at the pinned preset/crf over identical input frames emits identical output bytes; the byte-exact proof forces libx264 (make check-cut, both hermetic synthetic grid incl. a speed-change segment + a crossfade join, and a real ear-approved corpus spot-check) since NVENC — the fast default — is not byte-reproducible run-to-run like every encoder op. The caller's render DIRECTIVES (crf quality, thread cap, cpu/hwaccel selection) cross because they affect the bytes and must reproduce for every caller; the ffmpeg flag construction (preset p6/medium, -tune, -hwaccel, the encoder arg strings) stays in the handler. NO cut DECISION is in these params by construction: the keep-list is the already-composed cut, not a threshold; the whole ASR→Director→dedup→pause/retake removal that PRODUCES it stays on the control plane.

keep.description: The [start, end] source-time spans to KEEP, in order — the already-composed, frame-snapped cut. WHICH spans is the editorial decision and it arrives as data; how they become a trim+concat render is the handler's. These are spans to keep, never a threshold.

fps_grid.description: The source video frame rate the keep boundaries were snapped to. Renders exactly nf=round(dur*fps_grid) frames per segment so V and A agree to the sample.

speed.description: The tempo factor F folded into this ONE render (video setpts/F, audio atempo=F per segment). DECIDED on the brain (speed factor); 1.0 = off, byte-identical to the un-sped path. Bounded to atempo's single-stage [0.5,2.0].

xfade.description: Equal-power room-tone crossfade at every speech join (s). 0 = butt-concat. Bounded to the trimmed gap and <0.45 of each adjacent range so no speech onset is faded.

crf.description: Intermediate quality (crf/cq). The cut is the FIRST of several chained lossy passes, so it runs at high quality; the FINAL delivery encode is downstream. A quality directive, not encoder flags.

threads.description: ffmpeg CPU thread cap. Affects libx264 output bytes, so it crosses and reproduces per caller; keeps the render gentle on the machine.

cpu.description: Force the libx264 rung (Mac / no GPU / the reproducible byte-exact proof). Default false = NVENC-with-degrade-ladder.

no_hwaccel.description: Disable NVDEC hardware decode of the source (decode-only, quality-neutral; off on the cpu path anyway).
